# Service Agreement Starter

This is a starter template for attorney review, not a final legal document.

## Parties

This agreement is between {{your_business_name}} ("Provider") and {{client_name}} ("Client") as of {{effective_date}}.

## Services

Provider will deliver the website services described in the accepted proposal or statement of work.

## Payment

- Client will pay the fees stated in the proposal.
- A non-refundable deposit of {{deposit_terms}} is due before work begins.
- Final payment is due before launch unless the proposal states otherwise.
- Late payments may pause the project.

## Client Responsibilities

Client agrees to:

- provide timely feedback,
- provide or approve final text, logos, photos, and other assets,
- confirm that Client has the rights to all assets supplied,
- review and approve the final website before launch.

## Revisions

The project includes {{revision_limit}} rounds of revisions. Work outside that limit may be billed separately.

## Content and Asset Rights

Provider may use public business information as research input for drafts and concepts.

Provider will not publish final work until Client has approved the content and confirmed that Client owns or has permission to use supplied assets, including logos, photographs, videos, and testimonials.

Client represents that any materials it provides do not infringe the rights of others.

## Intellectual Property

Upon full payment, Client receives the rights described in the proposal for the final deliverables, excluding Provider tools, reusable code, frameworks, processes, and other pre-existing materials.

## Third-Party Services

Web hosting, domains, plugins, fonts, booking systems, payment processors, and other third-party services may be subject to separate terms and fees.

Provider is not responsible for outages or policy changes caused by third-party vendors.

## Account Ownership and Handoff

The proposal or statement of work should identify whether the domain, hosting, and related third-party accounts will be client-owned or provider-managed.

Unless otherwise stated in writing, the preferred handoff model is that the Client controls the final domain registration, hosting access, and other core accounts after final payment, while the Provider may retain ownership of reusable tools, starter code, frameworks, and internal processes.

If the Provider temporarily registers a domain or sets up services on the Client's behalf, the agreement should specify when and how control will be transferred.

## Launch and Warranty

Provider will make reasonable efforts to deliver a functioning site consistent with the agreed scope at launch.

Any post-launch support period is limited to the period stated in the proposal, if any.

## Limitation of Liability

To the fullest extent allowed by law, Provider's liability will be limited to the amount Client paid under this agreement.

Provider is not responsible for lost profits, indirect damages, or claims arising from Client-supplied content or Client's business practices.

## Termination

Either party may terminate for material breach if the other party fails to cure after written notice.

If the project is terminated, Client will pay for work completed up to the termination date.

## Governing Law

This agreement is governed by the laws of the State of {{state}}.
